message:
feat: expand analysis, schema validation tests, and refresh documentation

Add schema validation tests (+72 lines), extend analysis and TUI modules,
update project documentation. Rotate stale beads history snapshots and
sync tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,8 +33,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","title":"Define the evidence standard for parity claims so beads can be closed honestly","description":"Purpose:
Define what counts as sufficient evidence for saying a surface is done. Some parity claims can be established by fixture-backed conformance; others need TUI journey coverage, shell e2e, or targeted regression tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Different evidence classes are spelled out for robot, TUI, export/pages, and workspace/hook surfaces.
- The plan distinguishes proof of implementation from proof of behavioral equivalence.
- Closing guidance is strict enough that we do not recreate the current overclaim problem.","notes":"Parity evidence standard:

A. General rule
- A parity claim is valid only when the tracker can point to both the implementation anchor and the proof anchor.
- "Implemented" is not enough. We distinguish: implemented, proven equivalent, partially proven, divergent-by-design, and unknown.
- If the behavior affects operator recovery, path resolution, or cross-mode/context continuity, pure happy-path testing is insufficient.

B. Evidence ladder for loader/model/path semantics
- Required minimum: focused unit tests for parsing/validation rules plus CLI-entry integration coverage for cwd/repo/workspace/beads-file resolution semantics.
- Needed when legacy behavior is subtle: adversarial fixtures or integration cases that exercise malformed input, missing files, ambiguous roots, and precedence rules.
- Closure bar: a future agent can tell which root/path semantics are guaranteed and which are merely inferred.

C. Evidence ladder for robot/CLI contract parity
- Required minimum: one deterministic proof layer near the implementation (unit/conformance/schema) and one proof layer from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Use conformance fixtures when legacy output shape/order/omission is the source of truth.
- Use schema validation when shape contracts matter beyond a single fixture.
- Use CLI integration/e2e when path/config resolution, error behavior, or multi-flag interaction matters.
- Closure bar: command family behavior, default behavior, filter behavior, and failure behavior are all evidenced at the right layer.

D. Evidence ladder for TUI workflow parity
- Required minimum: state/keyflow coverage for the interaction logic plus either snapshot or journey evidence for the user-visible workflow.
- Use snapshots when layout/detail visibility matters.
- Use keyflow/state tests when search/filter/focus/selection transitions matter.
- Use multi-mode journey tests when the operator workflow crosses views or depends on sustained context.
- Closure bar: we can explain not just that a key exists, but that the operator can complete the legacy job-to-be-done.

E. Evidence ladder for export/pages/workspace integration parity
- Required minimum: targeted unit/integration coverage for root-cause semantics plus shell/e2e proof for operator workflows with detailed logs.
- Required logging: cwd/repo/workspace context, replay command, stdout/stderr, and any preview/watch lifecycle artifacts relevant to diagnosis.
- Use failure-path cases deliberately: invalid saved config, broken hook, non-repo cwd, watch reload edge cases, preview path traversal protections, and deploy-target repair flows.
- Closure bar: both happy path and operator recovery path are evidenced.

F. Documentation truthfulness standard
- Docs may only claim parity at the strongest evidence level actually achieved.
- Additive Rust-only features must be labeled as additive, not counted as proof that a legacy workflow is covered.
- Intentional divergences must name the preserved user outcome and why the difference is acceptable.

G. Anti-overclaim rule
- A bead cannot close by citing only old markdown prose or broad suite counts. It must name the specific tests, fixtures, scripts, or artifacts that establish confidence for that surface.
- If evidence is missing but the code looks plausible, classify as partial or unknown until proof exists.

H. Diagnostic quality standard
- Tests and scripts used as parity evidence must fail loudly and locally. A future agent should be able to infer the broken workflow, command family, mode, path context, or fixture from the failure output without rereading the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.806036575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.805311652Z","close_reason":"Tracker now records the parity evidence standard, including required proof ladders and the anti-overclaim rule for closing beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":135,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Evidence standard recorded:
Parity closure now requires paired implementation and proof anchors, with explicit ladders for loader/path semantics, robot CLI contracts, TUI workflows, export/pages/workspace integrations, and documentation truthfulness. The anti-overclaim rule is explicit: no bead closes on broad suite counts or optimistic prose alone.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","title":"Record the fresh gap ledger with explicit statuses: proven, partial, unknown, divergent-by-design","description":"Purpose:
Translate the inventory and evidence standard into an actionable gap ledger. This is the gate between planning and implementation.

Requirements:
- Every material gap becomes a child bead in one of the execution tracks.
- Every currently claimed-complete surface is either revalidated or downgraded.
- Any intentional divergence must include why it is acceptable and what user outcome remains covered.","notes":"Fresh gap ledger snapshot:

1. Loader/model/root-resolution semantics
- Status: partial / high-risk.
- Why: recent real bugs in feedback storage, hook cwd, preview path handling, wizard repair routing, and repo-path/beads-file inference all came from this family.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

2. Core robot command families (triage/next/plan/priority/insights/diff/history/forecast/capacity/burndown/suggest/graph)
- Status: implemented and broadly proven, but not yet re-audited under the new evidence standard. Treat as provisionally partial until command-by-command audit confirms defaults, filters, omission rules, and failure behavior.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.2`.

3. Extended/additive robot surfaces (search, label intel, recipes, docs/schema, metrics, feedback, drift, correlation/impact, file-intel, briefs, sprint helpers)
- Status: mixed. Some are additive Rust improvements rather than strict legacy surfaces; others are claimed complete in docs but need truthfulness review so additive does not get confused with parity proof.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.5.1`.

4. Main TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at operator-workflow level. Existing snapshots and keybindings are not enough by themselves to prove users can complete the same browsing, prioritization, and detail-inspection tasks as in legacy `bv`.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

5. Board/insights/graph TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at workflow level. The modes are rich and heavily tested, but the new standard requires proof of operator jobs-to-be-done rather than mode existence or keybinding coverage.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

6. History-mode and cross-mode investigative parity
- Status: partial / high-value audit target. History is specifically vulnerable to overclaim because individual features can exist without the full investigative loop feeling complete.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

7. Export/pages/preview/watch/wizard workflows
- Status: implemented and actively tested, but still provisional under the new standard because happy-path coverage and feature presence do not automatically prove workflow equivalence or recovery-path clarity.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

8. Documentation and release-readiness claims
- Status: incorrect/overstated in current form. README and FEATURE_PARITY contain strong completion language that now must be treated as claims to be re-earned, not evidence.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.5.2`.

9. Divergent-by-design/additive surfaces
- Status: divergent-by-design where clearly Rust-specific and not required for legacy parity, but must be labeled honestly. Examples: Rust-only TUI modes, schema/docs outputs, some metrics/reporting differences.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1` plus whatever audit bead owns the adjacent workflow family.

Planning conclusion
- No additional top-level tracks are needed right now; the current `bd-2bpl.2` through `.5` split is sufficient.
- The first implementation-phase priorities should stay audit-first: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, and `bd-2bpl.4.3`.
- The strongest current downgrade is this: broad implementation exists, but exact capability parity is not yet proven under the new evidence standard.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.151000528Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.150434155Z","close_reason":"Fresh gap ledger recorded in tracker notes/comments, with provisional statuses and explicit mapping from each gap family to the current execution beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","gaps","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:21.706308818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:22.959815646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Gap ledger recorded:
The tracker now explicitly downgrades several surfaces from implicit "done" to provisional partial status until fresh audit evidence exists. The biggest current risk classes are path/root-resolution semantics, operator-workflow proof for the TUI, export/pages recovery-path behavior, and documentation truthfulness. No new top-level tracks are needed; the current execution tree is sufficient.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2","title":"Audit and close robot/CLI parity gaps against legacy bv semantics","description":"Background:
Robot-mode parity is the most objective and automation-critical surface. Even small path-resolution, filtering, metadata-shape, or loader-semantic mismatches can break downstream agent workflows.

Goal:
Systematically re-audit all legacy robot and CLI behaviors against the Rust port, then fix any remaining gaps in semantics, outputs, or workflow compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Loader, model, and path-resolution semantics are rechecked against legacy behavior.
- Every legacy robot command family has explicit parity evidence or a documented intentional divergence.
- Integration and conformance coverage exists for all reopened gaps.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.077749232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","title":"Re-audit loader, model, and path-resolution semantics against legacy behavior","description":"Purpose:
Audit the subtle compatibility layer that everything else depends on: `.beads` discovery, workspace/repo-path selection, warning suppression in robot mode, JSONL file precedence, BOM handling, malformed-line behavior, model validation, and status/dependency semantics.

Why this matters:
Many operator-visible parity problems are really path or loader mismatches in disguise. This bead should flush out those root causes before they continue leaking into downstream surfaces.

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T03:07:23.878703928Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: audited loader and path-resolution semantics against legacy worktree expectations and found a real parity gap in .beads discovery for linked git worktrees. Root cause: get_beads_dir() only searched ancestor .beads directories and did not fall back from a worktree .git pointer to the main repo .beads; the first implementation of that fallback also needed canonicalization of commondir to resolve dot-dot segments correctly. Fix landed in src/loader.rs: parse worktree .git, resolve gitdir, canonicalize commondir, derive the main repo root, and use the main-repo .beads when present. Added regression test get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree passed; prior rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets on this slice were green. cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide due existing unrelated drift in other files, but src/loader.rs was formatted with rustfmt --edition 2024.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:24:04Z"},{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: hardened the linked-worktree fallback in src/loader.rs after review identified an ancestor-scan correctness hole. Root cause: find_worktree_main_repo_beads_dir_from() used early-return option propagation inside the ancestor loop, so any malformed or non-worktree .git file in an intermediate ancestor aborted the entire scan before reaching the real worktree root. Fix: treat each ancestor independently, continue past invalid .git pointers or unusable commondir data, and only return when a valid main-repo .beads directory is found. Added regression test get_beads_dir_ignores_malformed_intermediate_git_file_during_worktree_fallback alongside the existing worktree fallback tests. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_ passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: the audit found a model-level validation gap in src/model.rs. Legacy validation requires updated_at to be greater than or equal to created_at when both timestamps are present, but Issue::validate() was not checking that invariant at all. Fix: reject issues whose updated_at is earlier than created_at while still accepting equal timestamps. Added direct unit regressions validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at and validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:47:34Z"},{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: found and fixed a CLI-to-loader parity mismatch around warning suppression. Root cause: loader warning suppression only consulted BV_ROBOT=1, but normal bvr --robot-* invocations did not set that environment variable, so malformed-line and invalid-issue warnings could still leak to stderr in ordinary robot mode. Fix: added loader-level robot-warning suppression state, wired main.rs to enable it whenever Cli::is_robot_command() is true before issue loading, and preserved the existing BV_ROBOT env behavior as an additional trigger. Added CLI integration regression robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env to prove malformed JSON warnings stay suppressed during normal --robot-triage usage. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:36:08Z"},{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit continuation (PearlMill): current loader/model/path code still covers deterministic JSONL selection, first-line BOM stripping, malformed-line suppression in robot mode, workspace discovery/namespacing, linked-worktree .beads fallback, and updated_at >= created_at validation. The `br ready` vs `bvr --robot-next` disagreement reproduced in this session is not a loader/path source-of-truth bug in this repo: `.beads/` currently only contains `issues.jsonl`. The immediate cause is triage-layer behavior in `src/analysis/triage.rs`, which excludes `in_progress` issues from recommendations, while `br ready` still lists the current P0 audit beads despite their `in_progress` status. That belongs under the robot-command audit/fix track (`bd-2bpl.2.2` / `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`), not as a new loader/model/path mismatch. Verification this session: `rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_` passed, `rch exec -- cargo test --lib parse_issues_from_text_strips_bom` passed, and `rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation diff_since_auto_robot_diff_suppresses_loader_warnings -- --exact` passed.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:41:49Z"},{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Progress update (BrightSpire): found and fixed a workspace auto-discovery path-normalization bug in src/main.rs. Root cause: workspace_discovery_start_points() mixed a relative --repo-path with an absolute current_dir, so the same .bv/workspace.yaml could be discovered twice under different PathBuf spellings and falsely trip the "workspace auto-discovery is ambiguous" guard. Fix: normalize relative repo_path values against current_dir before assembling discovery start points. Added regression test resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity in the src/main.rs test module, with a CurrentDirGuard to avoid leaking cwd on failure. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --bin bvr resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with existing repo warnings only. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:07:23Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","title":"Re-audit each robot command family for behavioral and output-shape equivalence","description":"Purpose:
Review every legacy robot family again with fresh eyes: triage, next, plan, insights, priority, diff, history, forecast, capacity, burndown, suggest, graph, search, label intelligence, recipes/scripts, docs/schema, and admin or operational flags.

Focus:
- Field shape and omission rules.
- Filter semantics and default behavior.
- Path/config resolution.
- Error/warning behavior and deterministic ordering.

Done means:
Every reopened mismatch becomes either a fix bead or a justified divergence note.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:51:40.052658285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:25.154059909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit start (PearlMill): used live `bv --robot-triage`, `bv --robot-plan`, and `bv --robot-next` to pick the next bead. All three identify `bd-2bpl.2.2` / the audit stream as the highest-impact current work. First concrete mismatch already surfaced: live `bv` currently treats the active P0 audit beads (status `in_progress`) as actionable/top-pick candidates and even returns `bd-2bpl.2.2` from `--robot-next`, while the current Rust triage implementation in `src/analysis/triage.rs` filters recommendations with `!issue.is_in_progress()`. That means Rust robot triage/next semantics disagree with the live `bv` prioritization surface for in-progress work. This is squarely a robot-command semantics gap, not a loader/path issue. I am continuing the audit from the reserved robot files and tests; `src/main.rs` is currently reserved by another agent, so I am avoiding edit collisions there until the conflict clears or I have a surgical fix that needs coordination.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:46:07Z"},{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit continuation (PearlMill): after the `in_progress` triage gap was fixed, I checked the remaining robot-surface audit matrix against live `bv` outputs and the current repo tests. Concrete finding: robot-command coverage is still uneven. Core families such as triage/next/plan/priority/history/diff/forecast/capacity/burndown have visible conformance/e2e presence, but several live robot surfaces appear materially less audited under the current evidence standard: search, label-health, label-flow, label-attention, recipes, and docs/schema.\n\nLive `bv` spot-checks from this session confirm these are real current robot surfaces with nontrivial schemas: `--robot-search` returns top-level keys `[data_hash, dim, generated_at, index, index_path, limit, loaded, mode, provider, query, results, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-health` returns `[analysis_config, data_hash, generated_at, results, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-flow` returns `[analysis_config, data_hash, flow, generated_at, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-attention` returns `[data_hash, generated_at, labels, limit, total_labels, usage_hints]`; `bv --robot-help` also documents `--robot-recipes`, `--robot-docs`, and `--robot-schema`.\n\nCurrent repo test inventory check: `tests/e2e_robot_matrix.rs` has explicit e2e coverage for docs/schema/metrics but not obvious e2e coverage for search or the label-intel families; `tests/conformance.rs` has broad legacy-core coverage but not obvious command-specific parity assertions for these additive surfaces. Audit implication: these commands should be treated as either (a) additive/divergent-by-design robot surfaces that need truthful documentation, or (b) surfaces needing stronger regression coverage under `bd-2bpl.2.4.1`, rather than being silently assumed covered at the same standard as the legacy-core families.","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:51:40Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","title":"Re-audit loader, model, and path-resolution semantics against legacy behavior","description":"Purpose:
Audit the subtle compatibility layer that everything else depends on: `.beads` discovery, workspace/repo-path selection, warning suppression in robot mode, JSONL file precedence, BOM handling, malformed-line behavior, model validation, and status/dependency semantics.

Why this matters:
Many operator-visible parity problems are really path or loader mismatches in disguise. This bead should flush out those root causes before they continue leaking into downstream surfaces.

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:50.577596657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: audited loader and path-resolution semantics against legacy worktree expectations and found a real parity gap in .beads discovery for linked git worktrees. Root cause: get_beads_dir() only searched ancestor .beads directories and did not fall back from a worktree .git pointer to the main repo .beads; the first implementation of that fallback also needed canonicalization of commondir to resolve dot-dot segments correctly. Fix landed in src/loader.rs: parse worktree .git, resolve gitdir, canonicalize commondir, derive the main repo root, and use the main-repo .beads when present. Added regression test get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree passed; prior rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets on this slice were green. cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide due existing unrelated drift in other files, but src/loader.rs was formatted with rustfmt --edition 2024.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:24:04Z"},{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: hardened the linked-worktree fallback in src/loader.rs after review identified an ancestor-scan correctness hole. Root cause: find_worktree_main_repo_beads_dir_from() used early-return option propagation inside the ancestor loop, so any malformed or non-worktree .git file in an intermediate ancestor aborted the entire scan before reaching the real worktree root. Fix: treat each ancestor independently, continue past invalid .git pointers or unusable commondir data, and only return when a valid main-repo .beads directory is found. Added regression test get_beads_dir_ignores_malformed_intermediate_git_file_during_worktree_fallback alongside the existing worktree fallback tests. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_ passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: the audit found a model-level validation gap in src/model.rs. Legacy validation requires updated_at to be greater than or equal to created_at when both timestamps are present, but Issue::validate() was not checking that invariant at all. Fix: reject issues whose updated_at is earlier than created_at while still accepting equal timestamps. Added direct unit regressions validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at and validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:47:34Z"},{"id":140,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: found and fixed a CLI-to-loader parity mismatch around warning suppression. Root cause: loader warning suppression only consulted BV_ROBOT=1, but normal bvr --robot-* invocations did not set that environment variable, so malformed-line and invalid-issue warnings could still leak to stderr in ordinary robot mode. Fix: added loader-level robot-warning suppression state, wired main.rs to enable it whenever Cli::is_robot_command() is true before issue loading, and preserved the existing BV_ROBOT env behavior as an additional trigger. Added CLI integration regression robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env to prove malformed JSON warnings stay suppressed during normal --robot-triage usage. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation robot_triage_suppresses_loader_warnings_without_bv_robot_env passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:36:08Z"},{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit continuation (PearlMill): current loader/model/path code still covers deterministic JSONL selection, first-line BOM stripping, malformed-line suppression in robot mode, workspace discovery/namespacing, linked-worktree .beads fallback, and updated_at >= created_at validation. The `br ready` vs `bvr --robot-next` disagreement reproduced in this session is not a loader/path source-of-truth bug in this repo: `.beads/` currently only contains `issues.jsonl`. The immediate cause is triage-layer behavior in `src/analysis/triage.rs`, which excludes `in_progress` issues from recommendations, while `br ready` still lists the current P0 audit beads despite their `in_progress` status. That belongs under the robot-command audit/fix track (`bd-2bpl.2.2` / `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`), not as a new loader/model/path mismatch. Verification this session: `rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_` passed, `rch exec -- cargo test --lib parse_issues_from_text_strips_bom` passed, and `rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation diff_since_auto_robot_diff_suppresses_loader_warnings -- --exact` passed.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:41:49Z"},{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Progress update (BrightSpire): found and fixed a workspace auto-discovery path-normalization bug in src/main.rs. Root cause: workspace_discovery_start_points() mixed a relative --repo-path with an absolute current_dir, so the same .bv/workspace.yaml could be discovered twice under different PathBuf spellings and falsely trip the "workspace auto-discovery is ambiguous" guard. Fix: normalize relative repo_path values against current_dir before assembling discovery start points. Added regression test resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity in the src/main.rs test module, with a CurrentDirGuard to avoid leaking cwd on failure. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --bin bvr resolve_issue_load_target_discovers_workspace_from_relative_repo_path_without_ambiguity passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with existing repo warnings only. export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-15T03:07:23Z"},{"id":146,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Found and fixed another workspace-path mismatch under the loader/path audit.

Problem: the correlation audit commands (`--robot-correlation-stats`, `--robot-explain-correlation`, `--robot-confirm-correlation`, `--robot-reject-correlation`) were still building `.beads/correlation_feedback.jsonl` from raw `--repo-path` / `.`. In workspace mode, that meant a nested repo path would bypass the resolved workspace root and miss workspace-level correlation feedback.

Fix:
- src/main.rs now routes the correlation feedback store through feedback_project_dir(&cli), aligning it with the existing workspace-aware persisted-state behavior.

Regression coverage:
- added cli integration test `correlation_stats_reads_feedback_from_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace` proving `--robot-correlation-stats` reads `workspace_root/.beads/correlation_feedback.jsonl` when `--repo-path` points inside a discovered workspace repo.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation correlation_stats_reads_feedback_from_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets`
- `cargo fmt --check` still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:38:52Z"},{"id":148,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"BrightSpire","text":"Fresh-eyes follow-up found another workspace-root persisted-state mismatch in sprint metadata flows.

Problem: sprint-bearing CLI surfaces were still loading `sprints.jsonl` from raw `--repo-path` instead of the resolved load-target project dir. That affected `--robot-sprint-list`, `--robot-sprint-show`, forecast sprint resolution, and burndown, allowing workspace-root sprint metadata to be skipped when invoking from a nested repo path under discovered workspace mode.

Fix:
- added `load_sprints_for_cli()` in `src/main.rs`
- routed sprint list/show, forecast sprint lookup, and burndown through the workspace-aware project dir

Regression coverage:
- added `sprint_list_reads_sprints_from_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace` in `tests/cli_model_validation.rs`

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_model_validation sprint_list_reads_sprints_from_workspace_root_when_repo_path_discovers_workspace`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets`
- `cargo fmt --check` still fails repo-wide on unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside this slice","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:50Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","title":"Re-audit each robot command family for behavioral and output-shape equivalence","description":"Purpose:
Review every legacy robot family again with fresh eyes: triage, next, plan, insights, priority, diff, history, forecast, capacity, burndown, suggest, graph, search, label intelligence, recipes/scripts, docs/schema, and admin or operational flags.

Focus:
- Field shape and omission rules.
- Filter semantics and default behavior.
- Path/config resolution.
- Error/warning behavior and deterministic ordering.

Done means:
Every reopened mismatch becomes either a fix bead or a justified divergence note.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:32.088301868Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:25.154059909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit start (PearlMill): used live `bv --robot-triage`, `bv --robot-plan`, and `bv --robot-next` to pick the next bead. All three identify `bd-2bpl.2.2` / the audit stream as the highest-impact current work. First concrete mismatch already surfaced: live `bv` currently treats the active P0 audit beads (status `in_progress`) as actionable/top-pick candidates and even returns `bd-2bpl.2.2` from `--robot-next`, while the current Rust triage implementation in `src/analysis/triage.rs` filters recommendations with `!issue.is_in_progress()`. That means Rust robot triage/next semantics disagree with the live `bv` prioritization surface for in-progress work. This is squarely a robot-command semantics gap, not a loader/path issue. I am continuing the audit from the reserved robot files and tests; `src/main.rs` is currently reserved by another agent, so I am avoiding edit collisions there until the conflict clears or I have a surgical fix that needs coordination.","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:46:07Z"},{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Audit continuation (PearlMill): after the `in_progress` triage gap was fixed, I checked the remaining robot-surface audit matrix against live `bv` outputs and the current repo tests. Concrete finding: robot-command coverage is still uneven. Core families such as triage/next/plan/priority/history/diff/forecast/capacity/burndown have visible conformance/e2e presence, but several live robot surfaces appear materially less audited under the current evidence standard: search, label-health, label-flow, label-attention, recipes, and docs/schema.\n\nLive `bv` spot-checks from this session confirm these are real current robot surfaces with nontrivial schemas: `--robot-search` returns top-level keys `[data_hash, dim, generated_at, index, index_path, limit, loaded, mode, provider, query, results, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-health` returns `[analysis_config, data_hash, generated_at, results, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-flow` returns `[analysis_config, data_hash, flow, generated_at, usage_hints]`; `--robot-label-attention` returns `[data_hash, generated_at, labels, limit, total_labels, usage_hints]`; `bv --robot-help` also documents `--robot-recipes`, `--robot-docs`, and `--robot-schema`.\n\nCurrent repo test inventory check: `tests/e2e_robot_matrix.rs` has explicit e2e coverage for docs/schema/metrics but not obvious e2e coverage for search or the label-intel families; `tests/conformance.rs` has broad legacy-core coverage but not obvious command-specific parity assertions for these additive surfaces. Audit implication: these commands should be treated as either (a) additive/divergent-by-design robot surfaces that need truthful documentation, or (b) surfaces needing stronger regression coverage under `bd-2bpl.2.4.1`, rather than being silently assumed covered at the same standard as the legacy-core families.","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:51:40Z"},{"id":147,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","author":"MagentaCove","text":"Audit slice update: the additive robot families are better covered than the earlier note suggested, but I found a real schema-contract mismatch. Generated single-command schemas for several versioned outputs omitted output_format/version even though the live payloads include them. Fixed in src/robot.rs by introducing a versioned schema-property helper and applying it to versioned command schemas (search, recipes, label-health/flow/attention, metrics, sprint-list/show, correlation-stats, orphans, file-beads/hotspots/impact/relations/related/blocker-chain/impact-network/causality/drift). Added schema regressions in tests/schema_validation.rs for representative commands. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --test schema_validation. cargo fmt --check still reports unrelated pre-existing formatting drift elsewhere in the repo, not in the touched files.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","title":"Close reopened robot/CLI mismatches discovered by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
This is the implementation bucket for concrete robot/CLI bugs exposed by the fresh audit. Do not close it based on planning alone. Populate it via child comments or follow-up beads that reference the exact mismatch and root cause.

Acceptance:
- Every fix includes targeted regression coverage.
- New path/config behaviors are covered from the CLI entrypoint, not only internal helpers.
- The final state preserves determinism and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.741578308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.047404675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.598234256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
This is now a coordination container. Actual implementation should usually land on `bd-2bpl.2.3.1` and/or `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, with each code change tied back to a specific audited mismatch.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","title":"Fix loader/model/path-resolution mismatches surfaced by the robot audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes for loader, model, and path-resolution mismatches discovered in `bd-2bpl.2.1`. Keep this bead focused on discovery/root-cause classes that affect all downstream robot workflows, not on per-command payload details.

Required scope:
- `.beads` and workspace discovery semantics.
- `--repo-path`, `--workspace`, and `--beads-file` precedence and project-root inference.
- JSONL precedence, BOM/malformed-line handling, warning suppression, and validation semantics.
- Model/status/dependency interpretation differences that can change graph shape or command output.

Acceptance:
- Root-cause fixes are implemented without reducing existing functionality.
- Regressions are covered with targeted unit and CLI-entry integration tests.
- Any intentional divergence is recorded explicitly in comments or follow-up beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.140207150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.139341113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","title":"Fix robot command/output/filter/default mismatches surfaced by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete robot-mode fixes from `bd-2bpl.2.2`: payload shape, omission rules, sorting/determinism, filter semantics, default behaviors, and operational/admin command compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Every landed change references a specific audited mismatch and root cause.
- Fixes preserve deterministic output and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly approved and documented.
- Existing features remain available; no simplification that drops capability is allowed.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.674097358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.673284591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
